title: Build the spike test harness
description:
# Build the spike test harness

## What

A scriptable runner that executes one curated spike task against one frontier model and produces a structured result.

## Inputs

- A task definition (from the curated set).
- A model identifier.
- The boot prompt.
- The PlexusModule stub registered into a TactusRuntime.
- Configurable max attempts (default 1 for first-try-success measurement; up to N for "iterations to working code" measurement).

## Per-attempt behavior

1. Compose system prompt = boot prompt; user prompt = task prompt.
2. Send to the model with `execute_tactus` as the only tool available.
3. When the model calls `execute_tactus`, run the supplied Lua through TactusRuntime + the stub PlexusModule.
4. Stream progress and results back to the model in the next turn.
5. Capture: tokens consumed (prompt + completion), tool definitions size, latency, model output, generated Lua, Lua runtime result, errors.
6. After the model returns a final answer (or hits max attempts), check against `expected_outcome`.

## Per-task output

Structured JSON record:
```
{
  task_id, model_id, succeeded_first_try, attempts_used,
  total_input_tokens, total_output_tokens, tool_definition_tokens,
  latency_ms, total_cost_usd,
  generated_lua_per_attempt: [...],
  errors_per_attempt: [...],
  failure_classification: "api_design"|"boot_prompt"|"language"|"fundamental"|null,
  full_transcript: ...
}
```

## Aggregate output

Across all task × model pairs, produce a CSV / Markdown summary table for the go/no-go report.

## Reproducibility

- Fixture data is deterministic.
- Model temperature: 0 where supported (some require non-zero — record actual setting).
- Random seed where applicable.

## Deliverable

`spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/harness.py` (or similar) with a CLI: `python harness.py --task <id> --model <id>` and `python harness.py --all`.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Progress: built the first validation harness skeleton at `spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/harness.py`.

Current capabilities:
- Loads all YAML task definitions into typed `SpikeTask` records.
- Validates unique IDs, required fields, non-empty API lists/tags, and first-try max attempts.
- Provides a deterministic `stub-oracle` adapter that exercises the fixture-backed PlexusModule rather than calling real model APIs.
- Records generated-Lua placeholders, API call logs, stream events, cost/budget data, transcript data, and structured check results.
- Implements outcome checkers for the curated task kinds: exact fields, contains fields, structured summary, streaming operation, async handle, budget-aware, structured error, HITL-required, and docs-discovery-then-action.
- Writes per-task JSON result files and aggregate `summary.csv` under `spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/results/<run-id>/`.
- Added `spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/results/.gitignore` so generated result files do not get committed accidentally.

Smoke validation:
- `python -m py_compile spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/plexus_module_stub.py spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/harness.py`
- `python spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/harness.py --validate-tasks`
- `python spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/harness.py --all --model stub-oracle --run-id local-smoke-3`

Final oracle result before cleanup: passed 15/15 curated tasks. A final ignored smoke run is being used to keep generated outputs out of the working tree.

Task remains `in_progress` pending review/landing and later real model adapters.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Progress update: extended the harness beyond `stub-oracle` so it can exercise real model-generated Lua.

Added:
- Lupa execution shim that provides `require("plexus")` and converts Python fixture data into Lua tables (supports ordinary `ipairs` over returned lists).
- Recursive Lua-table to Python conversion for final results.
- Lua extraction from fenced or raw model output.
- Provider adapters:
  - `anthropic:<model>` and `claude-*`
  - `openai:<model>` and `gpt-*`
  - `litellm:<model>` and `gemini-*` via LiteLLM
- `model_prompt()` that combines `boot_prompt.md` with each task prompt and asks for Lua code only.
- `spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/README.md` documenting local smoke tests, provider model runs, and required environment variables.

Validation:
- Direct Lua execution against the stub works with code shaped like the boot prompt examples (`ipairs(cards)`, nested score inspection, `return { ... }`).
- `python -m py_compile spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/plexus_module_stub.py spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/harness.py`
- `python spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/harness.py --validate-tasks`
- `python spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/harness.py --all --model stub-oracle --run-id ignored-smoke-2`
- Result: passed 15/15 curated tasks.

No real model calls have been run yet; provider adapters are wired but await credentials / explicit run instruction.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Progress update: added real-provider readiness support to the harness.

Changes:
- Added `--check-providers` to `spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/harness.py`.
- Readiness check reports installed provider packages and env var presence without printing secret values.
- Added documentation in `spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/README.md` for safe provider readiness checks and the recommended one-task low-cost run before any full matrix run.

Verification commands run:
```bash
python -m py_compile spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/plexus_module_stub.py spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/harness.py
python spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/harness.py --validate-tasks
python spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/harness.py --check-providers
python spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/harness.py --all --model stub-oracle --run-id ignored-smoke-readiness
```

Results:
- Task validation: 15 tasks valid.
- Provider packages present: anthropic=true, openai=true, litellm=true.
- Provider credentials missing: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY, GOOGLE_API_KEY, GEMINI_API_KEY, VERTEXAI_PROJECT, AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID all absent.
- Stub oracle smoke: passed 15/15.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Follow-up hardening: added a real-provider full-matrix safety guard to the harness.

Changes:
- Added provider readiness preflight before paid model calls.
- Added `--allow-full-matrix` flag.
- The harness now refuses `--all` with any real provider unless `--allow-full-matrix` is explicitly passed.
- README now documents the required sequence: `--check-providers`, one-task readiness run, then explicit `--allow-full-matrix` only after the readiness run succeeds.

Verification:
```bash
python -m py_compile "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/plexus_module_stub.py" "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/harness.py" "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/report.py"
python "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/harness.py" --validate-tasks
python "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/harness.py" --all --model stub-oracle --run-id ignored-parent-verify
python "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/report.py" --run-id ignored-parent-verify
python "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/harness.py" --all --model litellm:dummy-model --run-id should-not-run 2>&1 || true
```

Results:
- Task validation: 15 valid.
- Stub oracle: 15/15 passed.
- Report scaffold generated from ignored smoke results.
- Unsafe full real-provider run was refused before any provider call.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Added multi-model matrix support to the spike harness.

Changes:
- New `--models` CLI option accepts comma-separated model IDs.
- `--model` and `--models` are mutually exclusive.
- Duplicate model IDs are rejected to avoid per-task JSON artifact collisions.
- Existing full-matrix guard now applies across the full model list: `--all` with any real provider still requires explicit `--allow-full-matrix`.
- README now documents one-task multi-model readiness runs and full explicit matrix usage.

Verification:
```bash
python -m py_compile "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/harness.py"
python "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/harness.py" --task predict_single_item --models stub-oracle --run-id ignored-models-verify-2
python "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/harness.py" --task predict_single_item --models stub-oracle,stub-oracle --run-id should-not-run 2>&1 || true
python "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/harness.py" --all --models stub-oracle,litellm:dummy-model --run-id should-not-run 2>&1 || true
```

Results:
- Single-model list path passed 1/1.
- Duplicate model list was rejected.
- Mixed stub+real full matrix was rejected without `--allow-full-matrix` before any provider call.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Added focused pytest coverage for spike harness/report guardrails.

New test file:
- `spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/test_harness_report.py`

Covered behavior:
- `--models` rejects duplicate model IDs.
- `--model` and `--models` are mutually exclusive.
- Pre-dispatch cost cap refuses over-cap real-provider plans before provider calls.
- Stub oracle calls do not count against real-provider cost estimates.
- Report decision gate remains pending for four real models that only ran one task.
- Report decision gate becomes ready only when four real models each have complete curated-task coverage.

Verification:
```bash
python -m pytest "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/test_harness_report.py" -q
python -m py_compile "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/plexus_module_stub.py" "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/harness.py" "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/report.py" "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/measure_mcp_catalog.py"
python "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/harness.py" --validate-tasks
python "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/harness.py" --all --model stub-oracle --run-id ignored-tests-verify
```

Results:
- Guardrail tests: 6 passed.
- Task validation: 15 valid.
- Stub oracle: 15/15 passed.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Improved token accounting for real-provider runs.

Changes:
- Added `normalize_provider_usage(transcript)` to `harness.py`.
- Supports common provider usage shapes:
  - Anthropic/OpenAI-style `input_tokens` / `output_tokens`
  - LiteLLM-style `prompt_tokens` / `completion_tokens` / `total_tokens`
  - Derives missing input or output from `total_tokens` when one side is present.
- `run_task()` now prefers provider-reported input/output token counts and only falls back to estimates when usage is missing.
- Added tests for Anthropic-style usage, LiteLLM-style usage, and derivation from total tokens.

Verification:
```bash
python -m pytest "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/test_harness_report.py" -q
python -m py_compile "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/plexus_module_stub.py" "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/harness.py" "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/report.py" "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/measure_mcp_catalog.py"
python "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/harness.py" --validate-tasks
python "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/harness.py" --all --model stub-oracle --run-id ignored-usage-verify
```

Results:
- Guardrail tests: 9 passed.
- Task validation: 15 valid.
- Stub oracle: 15/15 passed.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Continuing harness work after provider readiness checks selected `openai:gpt-5.4-mini` as the standard spike model. Immediate fixes: OpenAI provider calls must not send unsupported parameters like `temperature=0` to models that reject them, and provider/API setup errors must be classified separately from prompt/model/code failures. After focused tests pass, run the full 15-task spike on `openai:gpt-5.4-mini` and record results for the go/no-go report.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Harness continuation results:

- Adopted `openai:gpt-5.4-mini` as the standard spike model for the next validation pass.
- Fixed OpenAI provider calls to omit unsupported `temperature=0` for models that reject it.
- Fixed failure classification so provider/API request failures classify as `provider_error` instead of prompt/model failures.
- Fixed result artifact serialization so Lua-returned Python-backed structured errors do not abort the full run.
- Verification: `python -m pytest spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/test_harness_report.py -q` -> 22 passed; task validation passed; stub-oracle full run passed 15/15.
- Full standard-model run: `provider-full-openai-gpt54-mini-002` completed, `openai:gpt-5.4-mini` passed 1/15 first try. Report written to `spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/results/provider-full-openai-gpt54-mini-002/report.md`.

Interpretation: the single prediction task is solid, but the broader curated task set still has hidden task/checker/API-contract expectations. Most failures are classified as `boot_prompt`; one is a Lua syntax issue (`until` reserved word), and one exposed an API bridge issue around async evaluation/nested Lua table args. Next work should align all task prompts/checkers and add the bounded syntax/validation repair loop before another paid full pass.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Starting the next repair pass after `provider-full-openai-gpt54-mini-002` showed 1/15 first-try success. Scope: remove hidden contracts from the curated task prompts, add prompt/checker consistency coverage beyond `predict_single_item`, and fix the Lua/Python bridge issue exposed by async evaluation/nested table args. No additional paid model calls until local tests, task validation, and stub-oracle smoke pass.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Contract repair pass checkpoint:

- Made all task prompts explicit about required API calls and output fields.
- Added an all-task prompt/checker consistency test.
- Fixed nested Lua table argument normalization in the stub so async evaluation args with nested filters no longer crash call logging/deepcopy.
- Fixed budget-aware cost comparison to accept structured cost payloads like `{ usd = ... }`.
- Added focused tests for the async bridge and structured cost handling.
- Verification: `python -m pytest spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/test_harness_report.py -q` -> 25 passed; task validation passed; stub-oracle 15/15 passed.

Provider results:
- `provider-full-openai-gpt54-mini-contractfix-002`: 14/15 first-try pass.
- `provider-hitl-openai-gpt54-mini-contractfix-001`: isolated HITL task passed after making candidate_better explicit.
- `provider-feedback-openai-gpt54-mini-contractfix-001`: isolated feedback-summary task passed after clarifying feedback.find returns an array and uses `kind = "FN"`.
- `provider-full-openai-gpt54-mini-contractfix-004`: 13/15 first-try pass. Remaining failures: feedback summary omitted representative_item_id on placeholder patterns; dataset docs task emitted the correct Lua program twice, causing LuaSyntaxError.

Interpretation: prompt/contract repair moved the standard model from 1/15 to 13-14/15. The remaining work is no longer broad API comprehension; it is bounded validation/repair feedback: syntax validation, clear checker errors, and one or two repair attempts while preserving first-try metrics separately.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Starting bounded repair-loop implementation. Goal: keep first-try success metrics intact, but allow optional syntax/checker repair attempts with structured feedback so the spike can separately measure first-try generation and recoverable generation. No provider rerun until focused tests and stub-oracle verification pass.
